page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 32 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core, providing dedicated compute resources. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates solid mid-range performance across various workloads. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1865.39 and a multi-core score of 30983.52. In memory bandwidth testing, it recorded 1689.81 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 141.73 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, alongside a write speed of 65.94 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the system demonstrated strong text generation performance during LLM inference with a small 135M model, reaching 282.15 tokens/sec, while medium 7B model text generation operated at 17.54 tokens/sec. Database operations on Passmark scored 13455.54, and Redis SET operations reached 30.13 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.r7.8xlarge is the complete absence of local storage, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. However, the high memory density relative to dedicated CPU cores offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications that do not require expensive GPU hardware. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz with the x86_64 architecture. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 8.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place its performance in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 30983.52 and a Passmark database operations score of 13455.54. It shows strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 282.15 tokens/sec. Given its high memory-to-CPU ratio and lack of local storage, the instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that utilize network-attached storage, making it suitable for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized dedicated instance featuring a high memory-to-CPU ratio and 16 Gbps network bandwidth for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

256.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
